objective: Implement a dependency-free, explicitly bounded XML 1.0 character-data representation value over accepted XmlText, using a conservative candidate-owned escaping policy that emits ampersand, less-than, and greater-than as predefined references while preserving all other legal text, without claiming liblsl byte compatibility or opening document, parser, protocol, or runtime behavior.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the candidate-owned character-data representation policy and keep document, LSL mapping, oracle, protocol, and runtime authority closed.
Describe the bounded local representation and its evidence limit without presenting it as exact liblsl serialization.
Review whether candidate-owned representation policy adds reusable contract guidance; retain the shared skill unchanged if its value/representation and authority rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the clean-source and public-evidence boundary; retain the shared router unchanged if existing provenance guidance is sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: XML element, attribute, namespace, declaration, comment, processing-instruction, CDATA-section, entity-declaration, parser, decoder, canonicalization, or complete document APIs.

Generic entity or character-reference selection beyond the fixed candidate-owned &, <, and > character-data policy; in-place mutation of XmlText; or acceptance of text outside the LSLC-001B legal-character contract.

LSL stream-info field mapping, element order, empty-element form, whitespace layout, numeric spelling, channel-format spelling, exact endpoint bytes, oracle capture, candidate differential comparison, protocol-version behavior, or ecosystem compatibility.

Runtime identity, discovery, resolution, networking, sockets, inlets, outlets, transport, buffering, queues, threads, clocks, scheduling, recovery, providers, FFI, native loading, adapters, or language bindings.

Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, production liblsl integration, device validation, or Morphospace runtime authority.

acceptance.proof: A public XmlCharacterData value is constructed only from borrowed validated XmlText and emits every ampersand as &, every less-than as <, and every greater-than as >. This conservative local policy prevents a literal CDATA close delimiter, double-escapes caller text that already resembles a reference so its literal value is retained, and preserves legal whitespace, quotes, apostrophes, and non-ASCII scalars unchanged.

A separate validated nonzero UTF-8 byte-output maximum is checked against an overflow-safe exact encoded-length calculation before allocation. Exact-limit output succeeds, one-past output returns stable expected/required byte counts, arithmetic overflow and allocation failure have typed non-panicking errors, and accepted output exposes borrowed and consuming access with private state.

A separate LSLC-001C local-results overlay binds focused positive and damaged tests to the accepted LSLC-001A character-data role and LSLC-001B value contract while every LSLC-001A oracle/candidate role remains not-observed with null evidence. The overlay labels the fixed escaping choice as candidate-owned local policy, not observed liblsl behavior.

The crate remains dependency-, feature-, unsafe-, I/O-, and activation-free; historical evidence remains unchanged; public APIs open no parser, complete document, LSL mapping, protocol, wire, transport, adapter, provider, or runtime surface.

outputs: Dependency-free XmlCharacterDataLimit, XmlCharacterData, and typed XmlCharacterDataError APIs over borrowed validated XmlText.
Exact escaped-byte calculation and conservative fixed character-data representation with positive, damaged, allocation-retention, and error-precedence tests.
A separate LSLC-001C local-results overlay, deterministic focused checker, aggregate-gate routing, and lifecycle-neutral public documentation.
commit_policy: Commit the accepted character-data representation separately from element/document assembly, LSL field mapping, oracle capture, compatibility comparison, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, or runtime work.
